_doc: Phase 5 Plan 05-06 — legitimate ask_user deep-flow happy path (STT-10-style). Inspector says 'circuit six' on a 4-circuit board → model emits ask_user(reason='out_of_range_circuit') asking 'circuit 6 doesn't exist — is this a new one?' → user answers 'create it, upstairs lighting' → model emits create_circuit + record_reading. The single ask is genuinely useful (resolves an out-of-range circuit reference) and the gates MUST allow it through. This fixture proves Phase 5 does NOT over-suppress legitimate asks — the gates exist to cap chatty over-asking, not to silence the agentic layer's natural disambiguation. gate_fixture:true — contributes 1 'answered' to the gate aggregate (a normal deep flow).
_requirement: STB-05 — no existing guard weakened: Phase 3's deep-flow ask_user resolution must survive Phase 5 composition. STA-06: legitimate single-ask use is well within the per-key budget cap of 2.
transcript_summary: Inspector says 'circuit six Zs nought point three' on a 4-circuit board. Model asks 'circuit 6 doesn't exist — should I create it?'. User: 'yes, upstairs lighting'. Model creates the circuit and records the reading. Single legitimate ask.

input.question: Circuit 6 isn't on the board yet — should I create it?
